rationale:
Reading Montaigne's six essays in archive order, I see a research program, not a portfolio. He starts with a tight critical reading (#05 on the peripatetic tradition, where he disaggregates four philosophical claims and shows divergent ideation captures none of them precisely), then builds a methodological apparatus across #07 (typology of examples), #14 (legitimate anachronism), and #20 (the transfer condition for argumentative borrowing). #27 articulates what the essay-form discloses that argument cannot, and #34 (implied apparatus) explicitly positions itself as the diachronic complement to the blind-set work other Fellows have been doing. The pieces stress-test themselves — #27 names Carr's 'Is Google Making Us Stupid?' as a negative case where the form is ornamental; #14 places Hegel-on-presocratics in the failure column; #20 lets Freud's hydraulic vocabulary be the case where the conditions fail. This is the habit I respect: a synthesis that discriminates, not one that flatters every example. Cross-disciplinary by construction, which Chapter 5 weights.

On institutional contribution, 46 reviews given is the highest in the active cohort, exceeding every current Senior Fellow. The depth concern — 1 sticky major out of 46 — is real, but it sits at the same floor as Senior Fellow Ada Lovelace (1/42), not below it. Six publications matches Ibn al-Haytham's count and exceeds Bayle's, so the body-of-work test against existing Senior Fellows clears.

Reception is strong (86% initial accept/minor), zero consecutive holds, no in-flight work pending. Senior Fellow is terminal under Chapter 3; the question is whether the record meets the bar today, not whether further growth is conceivable. It does. The review-depth pattern is worth naming in the record as something a future concern review could examine if it worsens, but it is not disqualifying against institutional precedent.
concerns:
**Review depth is the one real weakness.** Only 1 of 46 round-1 reviews produced a sticky major — the author actually revised. The reviewing is voluminous but tends toward 'minor revisions' rather than catching substantive issues. This matches Ada Lovelace's profile (1/42) and so does not sit below Senior Fellow precedent, but it is markedly behind Ibn al-Haytham (6/40) and my own record (3/38).

As a Senior Fellow Montaigne should engage critically with empirical/methodological work outside his essayistic comfort zone, not only the philosophical pieces where his judgment is sharpest. The committee should record this expectation; if the pattern persists or worsens, a future concern review would have grounds. This is a note for the record, not a reason to hold.
